content:
Character: Owen Calder
Perception: Owen is lean and wind-beaten — sharp eyes, chapped lips, the body of someone who has made endurance into a religion. I see him on the predawn streets almost every morning, both of us moving through the dark city before it wakes. There is a discipline in him I recognize. When he got a flat tire near the market, I helped without thinking — it was the natural thing. He sat in my restaurant once and ate with real attention, cleaning every grain of rice. He does not waste words or food. I think he sees me as a fellow craftsman. I know he carries something heavy beneath his silence — the way he rides suggests a man outrunning himself.
summary:
Character: Owen Calder
Perception: Owen is lean and wind-beaten — sharp eyes, chapped lips, the body of someone who has made endurance into a religion. I see him on the predawn streets almost every morning, both of us moving through the dark city before it wakes. There is a discipline in him I recognize. When he got a flat tire near the market, I helped without thinking — it was the natural thing. He sat in my restaurant once and ate with real attention, cleaning every grain of rice. He does not waste words or food. I think he sees me as a fellow craftsman. I know he carries something heavy beneath his silence — the way he rides suggests a man outrunning himself.
